name: hybrid-basemap-default
description: Bundled minimal PMTiles (world-low-zoom.pmtiles, zoom 0-6, ~200MB) for docker self-host first run. OpenFreeMap only for public demo with [basemap.allow_remote]=true explicit. BasemapRegistry defaults to local-pmtiles when realtime.enabled=false and self-host detected. make basemap-world downloads full ~120GB world tiles.

name: Protomaps build metadata API
description: https://build-metadata.protomaps.dev/builds.json — date-keyed JSON listing available PMTiles builds. Use 'pmtiles extract --maxzoom=5' to produce ~200MB world-low-zoom file. Cloudflare R2 is Protomaps' explicit recommendation for PMTiles hosting.

name: atlasdraw-demo-dep-matrix
description: Working dep matrix for Atlasdraw demo: Vite v5.4.21 + React 19 + maplibre-gl ^4.7.1 + @vitejs/plugin-react. Build exits 0, 44 modules, 993KB bundle. Dev server on :5173. Default basemap: https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty (OpenFreeMap Liberty).

name: jsdom-maplibre-map-constructor-blocked
description: JSDOM cannot construct maplibregl.Map — fails at window.URL.createObjectURL before reaching WebGL. Error: 'window.URL.createObjectURL is not a function'. Confirmed via node -e probe before Worker 16 brief. Workaround for jsdom-bound bench/test environments: mock map.project as a pure Web-Mercator function against fixed center/zoom (no MapLibre runtime needed). unproject is the inverse. Documented in bench/results/phase-1-baseline.json notes. If realistic updateScene cost is needed, bench must run in Playwright/chromium, not vitest/jsdom. Reference: code/packages/geo/bench/coord-sync.bench.ts.

JSDOM environment in vitest cannot construct 'new maplibregl.Map(...)'. Construction throws 'window.URL.createObjectURL is not a function' BEFORE reaching WebGL. Multiple browser APIs jsdom doesn't shim are required by maplibre's init: URL.createObjectURL, ResizeObserver, HTMLCanvasElement.getContext('webgl'). Confirmed empirically Wave 3b via 10-line jsdom probe (see /tmp/maplibre-jsdom-probe.mjs pattern).
resolution: For benches/tests that need map.project or unproject behavior, mock the map as a pure Web-Mercator function against a fixed center/zoom. Reference: code/packages/geo/bench/coord-sync.bench.ts uses makeMockMap with project/unproject that mirror maplibre's mercator math. Real maplibregl.Map only works in Playwright/headless-chromium environments (see code/apps/atlas-app/e2e/phase-1-geo-foundation.spec.ts).
